28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass
92	
93	
94	    @staticmethod
95	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
96	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \
100	            X, e, g = ctx.saved_tensors

2569	def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
2570	    import inspect
2571	    import types
2572	    from typing import Callable, Optional, Union, List
2573	
2574	    # And now re add our saving methods!
2575	    if model.push_to_hub.__name__ == "unsloth_push_to_hub":
2576	        original_push_to_hub = model.original_push_to_hub
2577	    else:
2578	        original_push_to_hub = model.push_to_hub
2579	    pass
2580	
2581	    signature = str(inspect.signature(original_push_to_hub)).replace("NoneType", "None")
2582	    signature = signature[1:]
2583	    signature = re.sub("<function save at .+?>", "torch.save", signature)
2584	    docs = original_push_to_hub.__doc__.encode("utf-8").decode("utf-8")
2585	
2586	    push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
2587	    """
2588	    {docs}
2589	    """
2590	    arguments = dict(locals())
2591	    del arguments["self"]
2592	    if "tags" in arguments and arguments["tags"] is not None:
2593	        assert(isinstance(arguments["tags"], (list, tuple)))
2594	        arguments["tags"] = list(arguments["tags"]) + ["unsloth",]
2595	    elif "tags" in arguments:
2596	        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]
2597	    elif hasattr(self, "add_model_tags"):
2598	        self.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])
2599	
2600	    if "commit_message" in arguments:
2601	        commit_message = arguments["commit_message"]
2602	        if commit_message is not None:
2603	            if not commit_message.endswith(" "): commit_message += " "
2604	            if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
2605	                commit_message += "(Trained with Unsloth)"
2606	        else:
2607	            commit_message = "Upload model trained with Unsloth"
2608	        arguments["commit_message"] = commit_message
2609	
2610	    if "commit_description" in arguments:
2611	        commit_description = arguments["commit_description"]
2612	        if commit_description is not None:
2613	            if not commit_description.endswith(" "): commit_description += " "
2614	            if "Unsloth" not in commit_description:
2615	                commit_description += "(Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
2616	        else:
2617	            commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
2618	        arguments["commit_description"] = commit_description
2619	
2620	    # Update model tag
2621	    if hasattr(self, "config"):
2622	        _ = upload_to_huggingface(
2623	            self, arguments["repo_id"], arguments["token"],
2624	            "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
2625	            old_username = None, private = arguments["private"],
2626	        )
2627	    pass
2628	
2629	    try:
2630	        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
2631	    except:
2632	        del arguments["tags"]
2633	        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
2634	    pass
2635	
2636	    if hasattr(self, "config"):
2637	        print("Saved model to https://huggingface.co/" + arguments["repo_id"])
2638	    pass
2639	    '''
2640	    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())
2641	
2642	    original_model = model
2643	    while True:
2644	
2645	        if original_model.push_to_hub.__name__ != "unsloth_push_to_hub":
2646	            original_model.original_push_to_hub = original_model.push_to_hub
2647	            original_model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, original_model)
2648	            if hasattr(original_model, "add_model_tags"):
2649	                original_model.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])
2650	            pass
2651	        pass
2652	
2653	        if hasattr(original_model, "model"): original_model = original_model.model
2654	        else: break
2655	    pass
2656	
2657	    # Add saving methods to top level model
2658	    if not vision:

2660	            # Counteract tokenizers

2662	            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
2663	            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
2664	            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
2665	            model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
2666	            model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)

2674	        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:
185	        W = layer.weight
186	    return W, bias

1786	    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.85,
1787	):
1788	    """
1789	        Same as .save_pretrained(...) except 4bit weights are auto
1790	        converted to float16 then converted to GGUF / llama.cpp format.
1791	
1792	        Choose for `quantization_method` to be:
1793	        "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
1794	        "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
1795	        "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
1796	        "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
1797	        "f16"     : "Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
1798	        "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
1799	        "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",
1800	        "q5_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q5_K",
1801	        "q2_k"    : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.vw and feed_forward.w2 tensors, Q2_K for the other tensors.",
1802	        "q3_k_l"  : "Uses Q5_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
1803	        "q3_k_m"  : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
1804	        "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",
1805	        "q4_0"    : "Original quant method, 4-bit.",
1806	        "q4_1"    : "Higher accuracy than q4_0 but not as high as q5_0. However has quicker inference than q5 models.",
1807	        "q4_k_s"  : "Uses Q4_K for all tensors",
1808	        "q4_k"    : "alias for q4_k_m",
1809	        "q5_k"    : "alias for q5_k_m",
1810	        "q5_0"    : "Higher accuracy, higher resource usage and slower inference.",
1811	        "q5_1"    : "Even higher accuracy, resource usage and slower inference.",
1812	        "q5_k_s"  : "Uses Q5_K for all tensors",
1813	        "q6_k"    : "Uses Q8_K for all tensors",
1814	        "iq2_xxs" : "2.06 bpw quantization",
1815	        "iq2_xs"  : "2.31 bpw quantization",
1816	        "iq3_xxs" : "3.06 bpw quantization",
1817	        "q3_k_xs" : "3-bit extra small quantization",
1818	    """
1819	    if tokenizer is None:
1820	        raise ValueError("Unsloth: Saving to GGUF must have a tokenizer.")
1821	
1822	    arguments = dict(locals())
1823	    arguments["model"]        = self

1825	    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
1826	    arguments["save_method"] = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit

3	"""

5	
6	This script is designed as a starting point for fine-tuning your models using unsloth.
7	It includes configurable options for model loading, PEFT parameters, training arguments,

10	You will likely want to customize this script to suit your specific use case 
11	and requirements.
12	
13	Here are a few suggestions for customization:
14	    - Modify the dataset loading and preprocessing steps to match your data.
15	    - Customize the model saving and pushing configurations.
16	
17	Usage: (most of the options have valid default values this is an extended example for demonstration purposes)
18	    python unsloth-cli.py --model_name "unsloth/llama-3-8b" --max_seq_length 8192 --dtype None --load_in_4bit \
19	    --r 64 --lora_alpha 32 --lora_dropout 0.1 --bias "none" --use_gradient_checkpointing "unsloth" \

21	    --warmup_steps 5 --max_steps 400 --learning_rate 2e-6 --logging_steps 1 --optim "adamw_8bit" \
22	    --weight_decay 0.005 --lr_scheduler_type "linear" --seed 3407 --output_dir "outputs" \
23	    --report_to "tensorboard" --save_model --save_path "model" --quantization_method "f16" \
24	    --push_model --hub_path "hf/model" --hub_token "your_hf_token"
25	
26	To see a full list of configurable options, use:
27	    python unsloth-cli.py --help

29	Happy fine-tuning!
30	"""

54	
55	    # Configure PEFT model
56	    model = FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model(

67	        loftq_config=args.loftq_config,

69	
70	    alpaca_prompt = """Below is an instruction that describes a task, paired with an input that provides further context. Write a response that appropriately completes the request.
71	
72	    ### Instruction:
73	    {}
74	
75	    ### Input:
76	    {}
77	
78	    ### Response:
79	    {}"""
80	
81	    EOS_TOKEN = tokenizer.eos_token  # Must add EOS_TOKEN
82	    def formatting_prompts_func(examples):
83	        instructions = examples["instruction"]
84	        inputs       = examples["input"]
85	        outputs      = examples["output"]
86	        texts = []
87	        for instruction, input, output in zip(instructions, inputs, outputs):
88	            text = alpaca_prompt.format(instruction, input, output) + EOS_TOKEN
89	            texts.append(text)
90	        return {"text": texts}
91	
92	    use_modelscope = strtobool(os.environ.get('UNSLOTH_USE_MODELSCOPE', 'False'))

105	        gradient_accumulation_steps=args.gradient_accumulation_steps,
106	        warmup_steps=args.warmup_steps,
107	        max_steps=args.max_steps,
108	        learning_rate=args.learning_rate,
109	        fp16=not is_bfloat16_supported(),
110	        bf16=is_bfloat16_supported(),
111	        logging_steps=args.logging_steps,
112	        optim=args.optim,
113	        weight_decay=args.weight_decay,
114	        lr_scheduler_type=args.lr_scheduler_type,

117	        report_to=args.report_to,
118	        max_length=args.max_seq_length,
119	        dataset_num_proc=2,
120	        packing=False,
121	    )
122	
123	    # Initialize trainer
124	    trainer = SFTTrainer(

126	        processing_class=tokenizer,

2414	    self,
2415	    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
2416	    tokenizer            = None,
2417	    save_method          : str = "merged_16bit", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
2418	    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
2419	    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
2420	    is_main_process      : bool = True,
2421	    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
2422	    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
2423	    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
2424	    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
2425	    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
2426	    save_peft_format     : bool = True,

2428	    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
2429	    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.75,
2430	):
2431	    """
2432	        Same as .push_to_hub(...) except 4bit weights are auto
2433	        converted to float16 with as few overhead as possible.
2434	
2435	        Choose for `save_method` to be either:
2436	        1. `16bit`: Merge LoRA into float16 weights. Useful for GGUF / llama.cpp.
2437	        2.  `4bit`: Merge LoRA into int4 weights. Useful for DPO / HF inference.
2438	        3.  `lora`: Save LoRA adapters with no merging. Useful for HF inference.
2439	    """

2442	            "Unsloth: You're not saving a tokenizer as well?\n"\
2443	            "You can do it separately via `tokenizer.save_pretrained(...)`"
2444	        )
2445	    pass
2446	
2447	    arguments = dict(locals())
2448	    arguments["model"] = self
2449	    del arguments["self"]
2450	    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
2451	    for _ in range(3):
2452	        gc.collect()

2514	      `torchao_config` (TorchAOBaseConfig): configuration for torchao quantization, full list: https://docs.pytorch.org/ao/main/api_ref_quantization.html#inference-apis-for-quantize

2523	    del arguments["self"]

74	    return BLOCK_SIZE, num_warps

190	def get_lora_parameters(proj):
191	    """
192	    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
193	    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
194	    """
195	    # For DPO or disabled adapters
196	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
197	    W = base_layer.weight
198	
199	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
200	    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
201	    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
202	        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)
203	
204	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
205	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
206	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
207	    pass
208	
209	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
210	    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
211	    adapter = adapter[0]
212	
213	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
214	    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
215	    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
216	    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
217	    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
218	    A = lora_A_linear.weight
219	    B = lora_B_linear.weight
220	    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
221	        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
222	    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
223	        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)
224	
225	    return (
226	        W,
227	        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
228	        A,
229	        B,
230	        proj.scaling[adapter],
231	    )
232	pass

261	    """
262	    If QAT is enabled, fake quantize the input activations.
263	    Otherwise, just return the input activations as is.
264	    Weights are fake quantized separately in `get_lora_parameters`.
265	    """
266	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
267	    activation_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "activation_fake_quantizer", None)

269	        X = activation_fake_quantizer(X)
270	    return X

276	    @torch.inference_mode
277	    def fast_dequantize(W, quant_state = None, out = None, use_global_buffer = False):
278	        # TODO: After adding XPU BNB support, check this function
279	        if quant_state is None: return W
280	        if type(quant_state) is not list:
281	            # New quant_state as a class
282	            # https://github.com/TimDettmers/bitsandbytes/pull/763/files
283	            absmax     = quant_state.absmax
284	            shape      = quant_state.shape
285	            dtype      = quant_state.dtype
286	            blocksize  = quant_state.blocksize
287	            offset     = quant_state.offset
288	            state2     = quant_state.state2
289	            absmax2    = state2.absmax
290	            code2      = state2.code
291	            blocksize2 = state2.blocksize
292	        else:
293	            # Old quant_state as a list of lists
294	            absmax, shape, dtype, blocksize, compressed_stats, _, _ = quant_state
295	            offset, state2 = compressed_stats
296	            absmax2, code2, blocksize2, _, _, _, _ = state2
297	        pass
298	        global XPU_STREAMS
299	        device = W.device
300	        device_index = device.index
301	        XPU_STREAM = XPU_STREAMS[device_index]
302	
303	        n_elements_absmax = absmax.numel()
304	        # Create weight matrix
305	        if use_global_buffer:
306	
307	            # Use same buffers for faster inference
308	            size = shape[0]*shape[1]
309	            global WEIGHT_BUFFERS
310	            global ABSMAX_BUFFERS
311	            WEIGHT_BUFFER = WEIGHT_BUFFERS[device_index]
312	            ABSMAX_BUFFER = ABSMAX_BUFFERS[device_index]
313	            if WEIGHT_BUFFER is None:
314	                WEIGHT_BUFFERS[device_index] = WEIGHT_BUFFER = torch_empty(size, dtype = dtype, device = device, requires_grad = False)

2431	    """
2432	        Same as .push_to_hub(...) except 4bit weights are auto
2433	        converted to float16 with as few overhead as possible.

Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a final report with all the key information:

## Unsloth Codebase - Save and Export Methods Report

### 1. Top-Level Directory Structure (2-3 levels deep)

```
unsloth/
├── __init__.py                 # Main entry point - imports save, models, chat_templates
├── save.py                     # CORE: 2600+ lines, all save/export logic
├── chat_templates.py           # Tokenizer saving logic
├── tokenizer_utils.py          # Tokenizer utilities (GGUF fixes)
├── trainer.py                  # Training utilities
├── import_fixes.py             # Pre-import fixes
├── _auto_install.py            # Auto-installation logic
│
├── models/                     # Model architecture specific code
│   ├── __init__.py             # Exports FastLlamaModel, FastLanguageModel, etc.
│   ├── loader.py               # Main model loading/initialization logic
│   ├── loader_utils.py         # Loading utilities
│   ├── llama.py                # Llama model implementation (2900+ lines)
│   ├── mistral.py              # Mistral model
│   ├── qwen2.py                # Qwen2 model
│   ├── qwen3.py                # Qwen3 model
│   ├── qwen3_moe.py            # Qwen3 MoE variant
│   ├── gemma.py                # Gemma model
│   ├── gemma2.py               # Gemma2 model
│   ├── cohere.py               # Cohere model
│   ├── granite.py              # Granite model
│   ├── falcon_h1.py            # Falcon H1 model
│   ├── vision.py               # Vision model integration
│   ├── rl.py                   # RL training patches
│   ├── rl_replacements.py      # RL replacement layers
│   ├── dpo.py                  # DPO trainer patches
│   ├── mapper.py               # Model component mapping
│   ├── _utils.py               # Utility functions
│   └── llama4.py               # Llama4 model
│
├── registry/                   # Model registry system
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── registry.py             # Registry implementation
│   ├── REGISTRY.md             # Registry documentation
│   ├── _llama.py               # Llama variants registry
│   ├── _mistral.py             # Mistral variants registry
│   ├── _qwen.py                # Qwen variants registry
│   ├── _gemma.py               # Gemma variants registry
│   ├── _phi.py                 # Phi variants registry
│   └── _deepseek.py            # Deepseek variants registry
│
├── kernels/                    # CUDA/Performance kernels
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── fast_lora.py            # LoRA kernels
│   ├── cross_entropy_loss.py   # CE loss kernel
│   ├── layernorm.py            # Layer norm kernel
│   ├── rms_layernorm.py        # RMS layer norm kernel
│   ├── rope_embedding.py       # RoPE kernel
│   ├── flex_attention.py       # Flex attention
│   ├── swiglu.py               # SwiGLU kernel
│   ├── geglu.py                # GeGLU kernel
│   ├── utils.py                # Kernel utilities
│   └── moe/                    # Mixture of Experts kernels
│
├── dataprep/                   # Data preparation utilities
│   ├── __init__.py

│
└── utils/                      # General utilities
    └── __init__.py
```

---

### 2. Key Files Related to Saving/Exporting

**Primary File: `/unsloth/save.py` (105,493 bytes, 2,600+ lines)**

This is the main hub for all save/export functionality. It contains:

#### Exported Functions (`__all__`):
```python
__all__ = [
    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
]
```

---

### 3. Main Entry Points & Method Organization

#### A. **Core Save Functions** (in save.py)

1. **`unsloth_save_model()` (line 205)**
   - **Purpose**: Primary save endpoint with method selection
   - **Save Methods**:
     - `"lora"` - Save only LoRA adapters
     - `"merged_16bit"` - Merge LoRA + save as float16 (GGUF compatible)
     - `"merged_4bit"` - Merge LoRA + save as 4-bit quantized
   - **Key Features**:
     - Memory-aware merge and save
     - Push to Hub integration
     - Handles 4bit dequantization and merging
     - Multi-shard support

2. **`unsloth_save_pretrained_merged()` (line 1321)**
   - **Purpose**: High-level wrapped method for models
   - **Wrapper**: Calls `unsloth_save_model()` internally
   - **Attached to**: Model objects as `.save_pretrained_merged()`

3. **`unsloth_push_to_hub_merged()` (line 1364)**
   - **Purpose**: Save and push merged model to Hugging Face Hub
   - **Attached to**: Model objects as `.push_to_hub_merged()`

---

#### B. **GGUF Export Functions** (in save.py)

1. **`save_to_gguf()` (line 952)**
   - **Purpose**: Convert model to GGUF format (llama.cpp compatible)
   - **Quantization Methods Supported**:
     - `"not_quantized"` / `"f16"` / `"bf16"` / `"f32"`
     - `"fast_quantized"` (→ q8_0)
     - `"quantized"` (→ q4_k_m)
     - `"q8_0"`, `"q4_k_m"`, `"q5_k_m"`, `"q2_k"`, `"q3_k_l"`, `"q3_k_m"`, `"q3_k_s"`, `"q4_0"`, `"q4_1"`, `"q4_k_s"`, `"q5_0"`, `"q5_1"`, `"q6_k"`, `"iq2_xxs"`, `"iq2_xs"`, `"iq3_xxs"`
   - **Workflow**:
     1. Installs llama.cpp if needed
     2. Converts HF model to GGUF 16-bit
     3. Applies quantization with llama-quantize
   - **Return**: Returns multiple quantized files

2. **`unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()` (line 1768)**
   - **Purpose**: Model method for GGUF export
   - **Attached to**: Model objects as `.save_pretrained_gguf()`
   - **Parameters**:
     - `quantization_method` - Select quantization type
     - `first_conversion` - Initial conversion format (default: same as model_dtype)
     - `tokenizer` - Required parameter
   - **Workflow**:
     1. Merges LoRA to 16-bit
     2. Calls `save_to_gguf()` internally
     3. Restores tokenizer BOS token handling

3. **`unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf()` (line 1951)**
   - **Purpose**: Export to GGUF and push to Hub
   - **Attached to**: Model objects as `.push_to_hub_gguf()`

---

#### C. **GGML (LoRA-specific) Functions** (in save.py)

1. **`unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally()` (line 2211)**
   - **Purpose**: Convert LoRA adapters to GGML format
   - **Attached to**: Model objects as `.save_pretrained_ggml()`
   - **Workflow**:
     1. Uses `save_lora_to_custom_dir()`
     2. Calls llama.cpp's convert-lora-to-ggml.py script
     3. Creates `ggml-adapter-model.bin`

2. **`unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub()` (line 2143)**
   - **Purpose**: Convert LoRA to GGML and push to Hub
   - **Attached to**: Model objects as `.push_to_hub_ggml()`

---

#### D. **TorchAO Quantization Function** (in save.py)

1. **`unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao()` (line 2502)**
   - **Purpose**: Quantize model with TorchAO and save
   - **Attached to**: Model objects as `.save_pretrained_torchao()`
   - **Workflow**:
     1. Merges LoRA to 16-bit
     2. Quantizes with TorchAO (default: Int8Dynamic)
     3. Saves quantized checkpoint
     4. Optionally pushes to Hub

---

#### E. **Generic Save Functions** (for vision/custom models)

1. **`unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged()` (line 2413)**
   - **Purpose**: Generic merged save for non-standard models
   - **Attached to**: Vision models as `.save_pretrained_merged()`

2. **`unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged()` (line 2456)**
   - **Purpose**: Generic merged push for non-standard models
   - **Attached to**: Vision models as `.push_to_hub_merged()`

3. **`save_to_gguf_generic()` (line 2276)**
   - **Purpose**: Generic GGUF export for non-standard models
   - **Attached to**: Vision models as `.save_pretrained_gguf()` and `.push_to_hub_gguf()`

---

### 4. Method Registration/Exposure System

#### **Main Patching Mechanism: `patch_saving_functions()` (line 2569)**

This function is called by all model loaders and dynamically attaches save methods:

```python
def patch_saving_functions(model, vision=False):
    # Wraps original push_to_hub with Unsloth enhancements
    model.original_push_to_hub = model.push_to_hub
    model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, model)
    
    # Add Unsloth-specific methods
    if not vision:
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged
        model.save_pretrained_merged = unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao
        model.push_to_hub_ggml       = unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub
        model.save_pretrained_ggml   = unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally
    else:
        # Vision models have limited export options
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged
        model.save_pretrained_merged = unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = save_to_gguf_generic
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = save_to_gguf_generic
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao
    
    return model
```

#### **Where `patch_saving_functions()` is called:**

1. **`unsloth/models/llama.py` (line 78, 2188, 2204, 2902)**
   - Called in FastLlamaModel.get_model_and_tokenizer()
   - Patches both model and tokenizer objects

2. **`unsloth/models/vision.py` (line 33, 702, 703, 918, 964)**
   - Called for vision model loading with `vision=True`

3. **`unsloth/chat_templates.py` (line 33, 2309)**
   - Called for tokenizer patching

---

### 5. Import/Export Chain

**How methods are exported to users:**

```
unsloth/__init__.py (line 263)
    ↓
    from .save import *
    ↓
    Makes available:
    - patch_saving_functions
    - unsloth_save_model
    - save_to_gguf
    - print_quantization_methods
    - create_huggingface_repo
```

**But more importantly, methods are attached directly to model objects:**

```
FastLanguageModel.get_model_and_tokenizer()
    ↓ (in llama.py)
    patch_saving_functions(model)
    ↓
    Adds methods to model instance:
    ✓ model.save_pretrained_merged()
    ✓ model.push_to_hub_merged()
    ✓ model.save_pretrained_gguf()
    ✓ model.push_to_hub_gguf()
    ✓ model.save_pretrained_ggml()
    ✓ model.push_to_hub_ggml()
    ✓ model.save_pretrained_torchao()
    ✓ model.push_to_hub()  (wrapped with Unsloth enhancements)
```

---

### 6. Supporting Functions

**Helper Functions in save.py:**

| Function | Line | Purpose |
|----------|------|---------|
| `print_quantization_methods()` | 109 | Display available GGUF quantization options |
| `check_if_sentencepiece_model()` | 116 | Check if model uses SentencePiece tokenizer |
| `_merge_lora()` | 159 | Core LoRA weight merging with 4-bit dequantization |
| `fast_save_pickle()` | 190 | Optimized PyTorch save wrapper |
| `_free_cached_model()` | 137 | Clean HF cache to save disk space |
| `fix_tokenizer_bos_token()` | 1586 | Fix BOS token handling for GGUF |
| `create_huggingface_repo()` | 1456 | Create HF repo if it doesn't exist |
| `upload_to_huggingface()` | 1495 | Upload files to HF with metadata |
| `_determine_username()` | 1436 | Extract HF username from repo ID |
| `install_llama_cpp_*()` | Various | Non-blocking installation of llama.cpp |
| `get_executable()` | 937 | Find llama.cpp executables |
| `save_lora_to_custom_dir()` | 2129 | Extract and save LoRA adapters |

---

### 7. Key Constants & Configuration

```python
# Quantization methods mapping
ALLOWED_QUANTS = {
    "not_quantized", "fast_quantized", "quantized",
    "f32", "bf16", "f16", "q8_0", "q4_k_m", "q5_k_m",
    "q2_k", "q3_k_l", "q3_k_m", "q3_k_s", "q4_0", "q4_1",
    "q4_k_s", "q4_k", "q5_k", "q5_0", "q5_1", "q6_k",
    "iq2_xxs", "iq2_xs", "iq3_xxs", "q3_k_xs"
}

# llama.cpp build targets
LLAMA_CPP_TARGETS = ["llama-quantize", "llama-export-lora", "llama-cli"]

# Model layer weights for quantization
LLAMA_WEIGHTS = (
    "self_attn.q_proj", "self_attn.k_proj", "self_attn.v_proj", "self_attn.o_proj",
    "mlp.gate_proj", "mlp.up_proj", "mlp.down_proj",
)

LLAMA_LAYERNORMS = (
    "input_layernorm", "post_attention_layernorm",
    "pre_feedforward_layernorm", "post_feedforward_layernorm",
    "self_attn.q_norm", "self_attn.k_norm",
)
```

---

### Summary: User-Facing Save Methods

After loading a model with Unsloth, users have access to:

| Method | Purpose | Location |
|--------|---------|----------|
| `.save_pretrained()` | Standard HF save (wrapped) | Patched in patch_saving_functions |
| `.push_to_hub()` | Standard HF push (wrapped) | Patched in patch_saving_functions |
| `.save_pretrained_merged()` | Save merged 16-bit weights | Added via patch_saving_functions |
| `.push_to_hub_merged()` | Push merged to Hub | Added via patch_saving_functions |
| `.save_pretrained_gguf()` | Export to GGUF format | Added via patch_saving_functions |
| `.push_to_hub_gguf()` | Export & push GGUF to Hub | Added via patch_saving_functions |
| `.save_pretrained_ggml()` | Convert LoRA to GGML | Added via patch_saving_functions |
| `.push_to_hub_ggml()` | Convert LoRA to GGML & push | Added via patch_saving_functions |
| `.save_pretrained_torchao()` | Quantize with TorchAO & save | Added via patch_saving_functions |

All methods are attached at model instantiation time through the `patch_saving_functions()` call in the model loader.

   - **Purpose**: Convert model to GGUF format (llama.cpp compatible)
   - **Quantization Methods Supported**:
     - `"not_quantized"` / `"f16"` / `"bf16"` / `"f32"`
     - `"fast_quantized"` (→ q8_0)
     - `"quantized"` (→ q4_k_m)
     - `"q8_0"`, `"q4_k_m"`, `"q5_k_m"`, `"q2_k"`, `"q3_k_l"`, `"q3_k_m"`, `"q3_k_s"`, `"q4_0"`, `"q4_1"`, `"q4_k_s"`, `"q5_0"`, `"q5_1"`, `"q6_k"`, `"iq2_xxs"`, `"iq2_xs"`, `"iq3_xxs"`
   - **Workflow**:
     1. Installs llama.cpp if needed
     2. Converts HF model to GGUF 16-bit
     3. Applies quantization with llama-quantize
   - **Return**: Returns multiple quantized files

2. **`unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()` (line 1768)**
   - **Purpose**: Model method for GGUF export
   - **Attached to**: Model objects as `.save_pretrained_gguf()`
   - **Parameters**:
     - `quantization_method` - Select quantization type
     - `first_conversion` - Initial conversion format (default: same as model_dtype)
     - `tokenizer` - Required parameter
   - **Workflow**:
     1. Merges LoRA to 16-bit
     2. Calls `save_to_gguf()` internally
     3. Restores tokenizer BOS token handling

3. **`unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf()` (line 1951)**
   - **Purpose**: Export to GGUF and push to Hub
   - **Attached to**: Model objects as `.push_to_hub_gguf()`

---

#### C. **GGML (LoRA-specific) Functions** (in save.py)

1. **`unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally()` (line 2211)**
   - **Purpose**: Convert LoRA adapters to GGML format
   - **Attached to**: Model objects as `.save_pretrained_ggml()`
   - **Workflow**:
     1. Uses `save_lora_to_custom_dir()`
     2. Calls llama.cpp's convert-lora-to-ggml.py script
     3. Creates `ggml-adapter-model.bin`

2. **`unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub()` (line 2143)**
   - **Purpose**: Convert LoRA to GGML and push to Hub
   - **Attached to**: Model objects as `.push_to_hub_ggml()`

1. **`unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao()` (line 2502)**
   - **Purpose**: Quantize model with TorchAO and save

#### E. **Generic Save Functions** (for vision/custom models)

1. **`unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged()` (line 2413)**
   - **Purpose**: Generic merged save for non-standard models
   - **Attached to**: Vision models as `.save_pretrained_merged()`

2. **`unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged()` (line 2456)**
   - **Purpose**: Generic merged push for non-standard models
   - **Attached to**: Vision models as `.push_to_hub_merged()`

3. **`save_to_gguf_generic()` (line 2276)**

### 4. Method Registration/Exposure System

#### **Main Patching Mechanism: `patch_saving_functions()` (line 2569)**

This function is called by all model loaders and dynamically attaches save methods:

```python
def patch_saving_functions(model, vision=False):
    # Wraps original push_to_hub with Unsloth enhancements
    model.original_push_to_hub = model.push_to_hub
    model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, model)
    
    # Add Unsloth-specific methods
    if not vision:
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged
        model.save_pretrained_merged = unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao
        model.push_to_hub_ggml       = unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub
        model.save_pretrained_ggml   = unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally
    else:
        # Vision models have limited export options
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged
        model.save_pretrained_merged = unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = save_to_gguf_generic
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = save_to_gguf_generic
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao
    
    return model
```

#### **Where `patch_saving_functions()` is called:**

1. **`unsloth/models/llama.py` (line 78, 2188, 2204, 2902)**
   - Called in FastLlamaModel.get_model_and_tokenizer()
   - Patches both model and tokenizer objects

2. **`unsloth/models/vision.py` (line 33, 702, 703, 918, 964)**
   - Called for vision model loading with `vision=True`

3. **`unsloth/chat_templates.py` (line 33, 2309)**
   - Called for tokenizer patching

**How methods are exported to users:**

```
unsloth/__init__.py (line 263)
    ↓

    ↓
    Makes available:
    - patch_saving_functions

**But more importantly, methods are attached directly to model objects:**

    ✓ model.save_pretrained_gguf()
    ✓ model.push_to_hub_gguf()
    ✓ model.save_pretrained_ggml()
    ✓ model.push_to_hub_ggml()
    ✓ model.save_pretrained_torchao()
    ✓ model.push_to_hub()  (wrapped with Unsloth enhancements)
```

### 1. **Main Files**

### 3. **Method Registration Pattern** (Line 2663-2674)

Methods are patched onto the model class using `types.MethodType`:

```python
def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    # ... setup code ...
    
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf, model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf, model)
            # ... other methods ...
    else:

        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic, model)

    return model
```

---

### 4. **Core Conversion Flow**

#### Flow Overview:
1. **User calls**: `model.save_pretrained_gguf(save_directory, tokenizer, quantization_method="q8_0")`
2. **Method merges LoRA and prepares model** via `unsloth_save_model()` with `save_method="merged_16bit"`
3. **Core conversion** via `save_to_gguf()` function
4. **Optional upload** to Hugging Face Hub

#### Key Functions Chain:

**`unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()` (1768-1948)** → Orchestrates the entire process:
```
1. Validate tokenizer is provided
2. Prepare arguments dict for unsloth_save_model()
3. Fix tokenizer BOS token issues
4. Install llama.cpp (non-blocking) if needed
5. Call unsloth_save_model() with save_method="merged_16bit"
6. Extract model dtype and type from config
7. Call save_to_gguf() for actual conversion
8. Create Ollama Modelfile (optional)
9. Push to Hub if requested
```

**`save_to_gguf()` (952-1318)** → Core GGUF conversion:
```
1. Validate model_dtype (float16 or bfloat16)
2. Normalize quantization_method to list
3. Check CUDA support for bfloat16
4. Normalize quantization methods (e.g., "fast_quantized" → "q8_0")
5. Install/find llama.cpp and conversion scripts
6. Convert HF model to initial GGUF format (f16/bf16/f32/q8_0)
   - Uses llama.cpp/convert_hf_to_gguf.py
   - Modifies metadata to mark as "Unsloth" converted
7. For each quantization method:
   - Call llama-quantize with appropriate settings
   - Output to format like "unsloth.Q8_0.GGUF"
8. Return list of all saved file locations
```

**`unsloth_save_model()` (205-520)** → Merges LoRA weights:
```
1. Validate token for Hub uploads
2. Handle different save methods (lora, merged_16bit, merged_4bit)
3. For merged_16bit:
   - Extracts 4bit weights from model
   - Dequantizes to float16
   - Merges LoRA adapters
   - Saves merged weights to save_directory
4. Returns (save_directory, old_username)
```

---

### 5. **Quantization Methods Supported**

From `ALLOWED_QUANTS` dict (lines 79-104):
- `not_quantized`: Same as model dtype
- `fast_quantized`: Maps to `q8_0`
- `quantized`: Maps to `q4_k_m`
- `f32`, `f16`, `bf16`: Full precision formats
- `q8_0`: 8-bit quantization
- `q4_k_m`, `q5_k_m`: Recommended quantizations
- `q2_k`, `q3_k_l`, `q3_k_m`, `q3_k_s`: Various K-quants
- `q4_0`, `q4_1`, `q4_k_s`, `q5_0`, `q5_1`, `q5_k_s`, `q6_k`: Other formats

---

### 6. **Helper Functions**

**`unsloth_save_model()`** (205):
- Merges LoRA into model weights
- Handles 4bit→16bit dequantization
- Saves merged model to directory

**`save_to_gguf()`** (952):
- Core conversion logic
- Manages llama.cpp installation
- Runs HF-to-GGUF conversion
- Runs quantization for each method

**`upload_to_huggingface()`** (1495):
- Creates HF repo if needed
- Uploads GGUF files to Hub
- Creates model cards
- Uploads tensorboard files

**`fix_tokenizer_bos_token()`** (around 1550-1615):
- Fixes tokenizer adding extra BOS tokens
- Modifies chat templates

**`create_ollama_modelfile()`** (1618):
- Generates Ollama Modelfile for compatibility

---

### 7. **Usage Example from CLI** (unsloth-cli.py, lines 141-160)

```python
# Save locally
if args.save_gguf:
    if isinstance(args.quantization, list):
        for quantization_method in args.quantization:
            model.save_pretrained_gguf(
                args.save_path,
                tokenizer,
                quantization_method=quantization_method,
            )
    else:
        model.save_pretrained_gguf(
            args.save_path, 
            tokenizer, 
            quantization_method=args.quantization
        )

# Push to Hub
if args.push_model:
    model.push_to_hub_gguf(
        hub_path=args.hub_path,
        hub_token=args.hub_token,
        quantization_method=quantization_method,
    )
```

---

### 8. **Key Design Patterns to Mirror for ONNX**

1. **Method Patching**: Use `types.MethodType()` to add methods to model class
2. **Two-Level API**: 
   - `save_pretrained_onnx()` for local saving
   - `push_to_hub_onnx()` for Hub uploading
3. **Preparation Step**: Call `unsloth_save_model()` with `save_method="merged_16bit"` to handle LoRA merging
4. **Modular Conversion**: Separate core conversion logic from orchestration
5. **Hub Integration**: Use `upload_to_huggingface()` helper for file uploads
6. **Tokenizer Handling**: Validate tokenizer is provided, handle special cases (BOS token, chat templates)
7. **Tag Management**: Add "unsloth" tag to uploaded models
8. **Arguments Dict Pattern**: Convert function locals to args dict, clean up specific keys, pass to helper
9. **Flexible Quantization**: Support multiple export options/formats (like different quantization levels)
10. **Registration in `patch_saving_functions()`**: Add both save and push methods for language models

---

### 9. **File Locations Summary**
- **Main implementation**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775346268806-l370rw/unsloth/save.py`
- **Registration point**: Lines 2663-2674 in `patch_saving_functions()`
- **CLI examples**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775346268806-l370rw/unsloth-cli.py` (lines 141-160)

This pattern provides a clear template for implementing ONNX export with the same user experience and API consistency as GGUF export.

- `q2_k`, `q3_k_l`, `q3_k_m`, `q3_k_s`: Various K-quants
- `q4_0`, `q4_1`, `q4_k_s`, `q5_0`, `q5_1`, `q5_k_s`, `q6_k`: Other formats

---

### 6. **Helper Functions**

**`unsloth_save_model()`** (205):
- Merges LoRA into model weights
- Handles 4bit→16bit dequantization
- Saves merged model to directory

**`save_to_gguf()`** (952):
- Core conversion logic
- Manages llama.cpp installation
- Runs HF-to-GGUF conversion
- Runs quantization for each method

**`upload_to_huggingface()`** (1495):
- Creates HF repo if needed
- Uploads GGUF files to Hub
- Creates model cards
- Uploads tensorboard files

**`fix_tokenizer_bos_token()`** (around 1550-1615):
- Fixes tokenizer adding extra BOS tokens
- Modifies chat templates

**`create_ollama_modelfile()`** (1618):
- Generates Ollama Modelfile for compatibility

---

### 7. **Usage Example from CLI** (unsloth-cli.py, lines 141-160)

```python
# Save locally
if args.save_gguf:
    if isinstance(args.quantization, list):
        for quantization_method in args.quantization:
            model.save_pretrained_gguf(
                args.save_path,
                tokenizer,
                quantization_method=quantization_method,
            )
    else:
        model.save_pretrained_gguf(
            args.save_path, 
            tokenizer, 
            quantization_method=args.quantization
        )

# Push to Hub
if args.push_model:
    model.push_to_hub_gguf(
        hub_path=args.hub_path,
        hub_token=args.hub_token,
        quantization_method=quantization_method,
    )
```

---

### 8. **Key Design Patterns to Mirror for ONNX**

1. **Method Patching**: Use `types.MethodType()` to add methods to model class
2. **Two-Level API**: 
   - `save_pretrained_onnx()` for local saving
   - `push_to_hub_onnx()` for Hub uploading
3. **Preparation Step**: Call `unsloth_save_model()` with `save_method="merged_16bit"` to handle LoRA merging
4. **Modular Conversion**: Separate core conversion logic from orchestration
5. **Hub Integration**: Use `upload_to_huggingface()` helper for file uploads
6. **Tokenizer Handling**: Validate tokenizer is provided, handle special cases (BOS token, chat templates)
7. **Tag Management**: Add "unsloth" tag to uploaded models
8. **Arguments Dict Pattern**: Convert function locals to args dict, clean up specific keys, pass to helper
9. **Flexible Quantization**: Support multiple export options/formats (like different quantization levels)
10. **Registration in `patch_saving_functions()`**: Add both save and push methods for language models

---

### 9. **File Locations Summary**
- **Main implementation**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775346268806-l370rw/unsloth/save.py`
- **Registration point**: Lines 2663-2674 in `patch_saving_functions()`
- **CLI examples**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775346268806-l370rw/unsloth-cli.py` (lines 141-160)

This pattern provides a clear template for implementing ONNX export with the same user experience and API consistency as GGUF export.

## Summary Report: Unsloth LoRA Adapter Implementation

Based on my exploration of the unsloth codebase, here's a comprehensive overview of how LoRA adapters are implemented and managed:

---

### 1. **Key LoRA-Related Files**

| File Path | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` | Core utility functions for accessing LoRA weights (`get_lora_parameters`, `get_lora_parameters_bias`) |
| `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` | Efficient LoRA forward/backward pass implementations and custom autograd functions |
| `/unsloth/models/llama.py` | Main model loader class `FastLlamaModel` with `get_peft_model()` and adapter patching |
| `/unsloth/save.py` | Save/merge functionality including `save_pretrained_merged()`, `merge_and_overwrite_lora()` |
| `/unsloth/kernels/geglu.py`, `/swiglu.py` | LoRA-aware MLU layer implementations |

---

### 2. **LoRA Adapter Structure & Rank/Alpha Parameters**

#### Default Configuration (from `FastLlamaModel.get_peft_model()` at line 2266-2271):
```python
target_modules = ["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj",
                  "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"]
r = 16                    # Rank (default)
lora_alpha = 16          # Alpha scaling factor
lora_dropout = 0.0       # Dropout (0 recommended for fast patching)
bias = "none"            # Bias configuration
```

#### LoRA Config Creation (line 2598-2617):
The adapter configuration is created via PEFT's `LoraConfig`:
```python
arguments = {
    r=16,
    lora_alpha=16,
    target_modules=["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", ...],
    lora_dropout=0.0,
    bias="none",
    task_type=TaskType.CAUSAL_LM,
    layers_to_transform=None,
    init_lora_weights=True,
    loftq_config={},
    use_rslora=False,
    modules_to_save=None,
}
lora_config = LoraConfig(**arguments)
model = _get_peft_model(model, lora_config)
```

---

### 3. **How lora_A and lora_B Weights are Accessed**

#### Direct Weight Access Pattern (from `/kernels/utils.py` lines 214-223):
```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """Returns (weight, weight_quant_state, lora_A, lora_B, lora_scale)"""
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", ("default",))[0]
    
    # Access adapter by name
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]         # LoRA A module
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]         # LoRA B module
    
    # Get weight tensors
    A = lora_A_linear.weight      # Shape: (rank, in_features)
    B = lora_B_linear.weight      # Shape: (out_features, rank)
    
    # Apply optional fake quantization for QAT
    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)
    
    return (W, quant_state, A, B, proj.scaling[adapter])
```

#### Fast Forward Pass (from `/kernels/fast_lora.py` lines 489-497):
```python
lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter].weight     # (rank, in_features)
lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter].weight     # (out_features, rank)
scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]          # Scaling factor (alpha/r)
W = self.base_layer.weight                      # Base weight

# Efficient computation: output = x @ A.T @ B.T * scaling
return LoRA_W.apply(x, W, QUANT_STATE(W), lora_A, lora_B, scaling)
```

#### Key Data Structure:
- **Module Structure**: Each adapted layer (q_proj, k_proj, etc.) has these attributes:
  - `proj.lora_A[adapter_name]` → Linear module with shape `(rank, in_features)`
  - `proj.lora_B[adapter_name]` → Linear module with shape `(out_features, rank)`
  - `proj.scaling[adapter_name]` → Float scalar = `lora_alpha / r`
  - `proj.base_layer` → Original frozen base layer
  - `proj.disable_adapters` → Boolean flag for enabling/disabling LoRA

---

### 4. **Saving Adapters - Method Signatures & Implementation**

#### Primary Save Method (line 1321-1361):
```python
def save_pretrained_merged(
    self,
    save_directory: Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer=None,
    save_method: str = "merged_16bit",  # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub: bool = False,
    token: Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process: bool = True,
    state_dict: Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function: Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size: Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization: bool = True,
    variant: Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format: bool = True,
    tags: List[str] = None,
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.75,
):
    """Saves model with three options:
    - "lora": Saves only LoRA adapters (smallest)
    - "merged_16bit": Merges LoRA into float16 weights (for GGUF/llama.cpp)
    - "merged_4bit": Merges LoRA into int4 weights (for DPO/inference)
    """
```

#### Generic Push to Hub (line 2456-2472):
```python
def push_to_hub_merged(
    self,
    repo_id: str,
    tokenizer=None,
    save_method: str = "merged_16bit",
    use_temp_dir: Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message: Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private: Optional[bool] = None,
    token: Union[bool, str, None] = None,
    max_shard_size: Union[int, str, None] = "5GB",
    create_pr: bool = False,
    safe_serialization: bool = True,
    revision: str = None,
    commit_description: str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.75,
):
```

#### Core Unsloth Save Function (line 205-232):
```python
def unsloth_save_model(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory: Union[str, os.PathLike],
    save_method: str = "lora",  # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub: bool = False,
    token: Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process: bool = True,
    state_dict: Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function: Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size: Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization: bool = True,
    variant: Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format: bool = True,
    # ... push to hub options ...
):
```

---

### 5. **adapter_config.json Structure**

Based on the PEFT LoraConfig parameters created at lines 2598-2617, the adapter_config.json contains:

```json
{
  "r": 16,
  "lora_alpha": 16,
  "target_modules": [
    "q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj",
    "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"
  ],
  "lora_dropout": 0.0,
  "bias": "none",
  "task_type": "CAUSAL_LM",
  "layers_to_transform": null,
  "init_lora_weights": true,
  "loftq_config": null,
  "use_rslora": false,
  "modules_to_save": null,
  "inference_mode": false,
  "peft_type": "LORA",
  "auto_mapping": {
    "base_model_class": "LlamaForCausalLM",
    "parent_library": "transformers"
  }
}
```

---

### 6. **Model Object State with LoRA Applied**

When LoRA is applied via `model.get_peft_model()`, the model becomes a `PeftModelForCausalLM` wrapping the base model:

```python
# Accessed via:
model = PeftModelForCausalLM(base_model, lora_config)

# Structure:
model.peft_config["default"]           # LoRA configuration
model.base_model                       # Original frozen base model
model.model.layers[i].self_attn.q_proj # Each module has:
    ├── .lora_A                        # Dict of adapters: {"default": Linear(rank, in_features)}
    ├── .lora_B                        # Dict of adapters: {"default": Linear(out_features, rank)}
    ├── .scaling                       # Dict of scales: {"default": lora_alpha/r}
    ├── .base_layer                    # Original frozen layer reference
    ├── .active_adapters               # List of active adapter names
    └── .disable_adapters              # Boolean flag
```

---

### 7. **Save Workflow for Different Methods**

#### For "lora" save method:
- **What's saved**: Only LoRA A/B matrices
- **Files created**: `adapter_model.bin` (LoRA weights), `adapter_config.json`
- **Size**: Very small (typically 2-5% of base model)
- **Usage**: Fine-tuning continuation, inference with PEFT

#### For "merged_16bit" save method:
- **What's saved**: Merged weights (base + scaled LoRA)
- **Formula**: `W_merged = W_base + alpha/r * (B @ A)`
- **Process** (line 295-303, 2386-2394):
  ```python
  if save_method == "merged_4bit":
      if hasattr(model, "merge_and_unload"):
          model = model.merge_and_unload()  # Merge LoRA weights
  ```
- **Files created**: Full `model.safetensors` or `.bin`, config.json
- **Size**: Same as base model (no size reduction)
- **Usage**: GGUF conversion, llama.cpp compatibility

#### For "merged_4bit" save method:
- **What's saved**: Merged weights converted to int4 quantization
- **Merging function** (line 2396-2408):
  ```python
  merge_and_overwrite_lora(
      model=model,
      save_directory=save_directory,
      save_method="merged_4bit",
      # ... other params
  )
  ```
- **Size**: ~25% of base model size
- **Usage**: Inference optimization

---

### 8. **Key Patching Functions**

#### Patch Saving Functions (line 2569-2668):
```python
def patch_saving_functions(model, vision=False):
    # Dynamically adds methods to model:
    model.push_to_hub_merged = unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged
    model.save_pretrained_merged = unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged
    model.push_to_hub_gguf = unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf
    model.save_pretrained_gguf = unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf
    model.save_pretrained_torchao = unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao
    model.push_to_hub_ggml = unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub
    model.save_pretrained_ggml = unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally
```

---

### 9. **How Merging Works**

**The merging logic** is delegated to `unsloth_zoo.saving_utils.merge_and_overwrite_lora()`, which:
1. Iterates through all layers with LoRA adapters
2. For each adapter: `merged_weight = base_weight + (alpha/r) * (lora_B @ lora_A)`
3. Optionally converts to target precision (float16, int4, etc.)
4. Replaces base weights with merged weights
5. Removes LoRA modules from the model

---

### 10. **Quick Reference: Accessing LoRA Weights**

```python
# Get lora A weight matrix
lora_A_weight = model.model.layers[0].self_attn.q_proj.lora_A["default"].weight
# Shape: (rank, in_features)

# Get lora B weight matrix
lora_B_weight = model.model.layers[0].self_attn.q_proj.lora_B["default"].weight
# Shape: (out_features, rank)

# Get scaling factor
scaling = model.model.layers[0].self_attn.q_proj.scaling["default"]
# Value: lora_alpha / r = 16 / 16 = 1.0 (by default)

# Check if adapters are enabled
is_enabled = not model.model.layers[0].self_attn.q_proj.disable_adapters

# Access active adapter name
active = model.model.layers[0].self_attn.q_proj.active_adapters[0]
# Usually: "default"
```

| `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` | Core utility functions for accessing LoRA weights (`get_lora_parameters`, `get_lora_parameters_bias`) |
| `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` | Efficient LoRA forward/backward pass implementations and custom autograd functions |
| `/unsloth/models/llama.py` | Main model loader class `FastLlamaModel` with `get_peft_model()` and adapter patching |
| `/unsloth/save.py` | Save/merge functionality including `save_pretrained_merged()`, `merge_and_overwrite_lora()` |
| `/unsloth/kernels/geglu.py`, `/swiglu.py` | LoRA-aware MLU layer implementations |

---

### 2. **LoRA Adapter Structure & Rank/Alpha Parameters**

#### Default Configuration (from `FastLlamaModel.get_peft_model()` at line 2266-2271):
```python
target_modules = ["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj",
                  "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"]
r = 16                    # Rank (default)
lora_alpha = 16          # Alpha scaling factor
lora_dropout = 0.0       # Dropout (0 recommended for fast patching)
bias = "none"            # Bias configuration
```

#### LoRA Config Creation (line 2598-2617):
The adapter configuration is created via PEFT's `LoraConfig`:
```python
arguments = {
    r=16,
    lora_alpha=16,
    target_modules=["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", ...],
    lora_dropout=0.0,
    bias="none",
    task_type=TaskType.CAUSAL_LM,
    layers_to_transform=None,
    init_lora_weights=True,
    loftq_config={},
    use_rslora=False,
    modules_to_save=None,
}
lora_config = LoraConfig(**arguments)
model = _get_peft_model(model, lora_config)
```

---

### 3. **How lora_A and lora_B Weights are Accessed**

#### Direct Weight Access Pattern (from `/kernels/utils.py` lines 214-223):
```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """Returns (weight, weight_quant_state, lora_A, lora_B, lora_scale)"""
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", ("default",))[0]
    
    # Access adapter by name
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]         # LoRA A module
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]         # LoRA B module
    
    # Get weight tensors
    A = lora_A_linear.weight      # Shape: (rank, in_features)
    B = lora_B_linear.weight      # Shape: (out_features, rank)
    
    # Apply optional fake quantization for QAT
    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)
    
    return (W, quant_state, A, B, proj.scaling[adapter])
```

#### Fast Forward Pass (from `/kernels/fast_lora.py` lines 489-497):
```python
lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter].weight     # (rank, in_features)
lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter].weight     # (out_features, rank)
scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]          # Scaling factor (alpha/r)
W = self.base_layer.weight                      # Base weight

# Efficient computation: output = x @ A.T @ B.T * scaling
return LoRA_W.apply(x, W, QUANT_STATE(W), lora_A, lora_B, scaling)
```

#### Key Data Structure:
- **Module Structure**: Each adapted layer (q_proj, k_proj, etc.) has these attributes:
  - `proj.lora_A[adapter_name]` → Linear module with shape `(rank, in_features)`
  - `proj.lora_B[adapter_name]` → Linear module with shape `(out_features, rank)`
  - `proj.scaling[adapter_name]` → Float scalar = `lora_alpha / r`
  - `proj.base_layer` → Original frozen base layer
  - `proj.disable_adapters` → Boolean flag for enabling/disabling LoRA

### 6. **Model Object State with LoRA Applied**

When LoRA is applied via `model.get_peft_model()`, the model becomes a `PeftModelForCausalLM` wrapping the base model:

```python
# Accessed via:
model = PeftModelForCausalLM(base_model, lora_config)

# Structure:
model.peft_config["default"]           # LoRA configuration
model.base_model                       # Original frozen base model
model.model.layers[i].self_attn.q_proj # Each module has:
    ├── .lora_A                        # Dict of adapters: {"default": Linear(rank, in_features)}
    ├── .lora_B                        # Dict of adapters: {"default": Linear(out_features, rank)}
    ├── .scaling                       # Dict of scales: {"default": lora_alpha/r}
    ├── .base_layer                    # Original frozen layer reference
    ├── .active_adapters               # List of active adapter names
    └── .disable_adapters              # Boolean flag
```

---

### 7. **Save Workflow for Different Methods**

#### For "lora" save method:
- **What's saved**: Only LoRA A/B matrices
- **Files created**: `adapter_model.bin` (LoRA weights), `adapter_config.json`
- **Size**: Very small (typically 2-5% of base model)
- **Usage**: Fine-tuning continuation, inference with PEFT

#### For "merged_16bit" save method:
- **What's saved**: Merged weights (base + scaled LoRA)
- **Formula**: `W_merged = W_base + alpha/r * (B @ A)`
- **Process** (line 295-303, 2386-2394):
  ```python
  if save_method == "merged_4bit":
      if hasattr(model, "merge_and_unload"):
          model = model.merge_and_unload()  # Merge LoRA weights
  ```
- **Files created**: Full `model.safetensors` or `.bin`, config.json
- **Size**: Same as base model (no size reduction)
- **Usage**: GGUF conversion, llama.cpp compatibility

#### For "merged_4bit" save method:
- **What's saved**: Merged weights converted to int4 quantization
- **Merging function** (line 2396-2408):
  ```python
  merge_and_overwrite_lora(
      model=model,
      save_directory=save_directory,
      save_method="merged_4bit",
      # ... other params
  )
  ```
- **Size**: ~25% of base model size
- **Usage**: Inference optimization

---

### 8. **Key Patching Functions**

#### Patch Saving Functions (line 2569-2668):
```python
def patch_saving_functions(model, vision=False):
    # Dynamically adds methods to model:
    model.push_to_hub_merged = unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged
    model.save_pretrained_merged = unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged
    model.push_to_hub_gguf = unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf
    model.save_pretrained_gguf = unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf
    model.save_pretrained_torchao = unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao
    model.push_to_hub_ggml = unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub
    model.save_pretrained_ggml = unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally
```

---

### 9. **How Merging Works**

**The merging logic** is delegated to `unsloth_zoo.saving_utils.merge_and_overwrite_lora()`, which:
1. Iterates through all layers with LoRA adapters
2. For each adapter: `merged_weight = base_weight + (alpha/r) * (lora_B @ lora_A)`
3. Optionally converts to target precision (float16, int4, etc.)
4. Replaces base weights with merged weights
5. Removes LoRA modules from the model

---

### 10. **Quick Reference: Accessing LoRA Weights**

```python
# Get lora A weight matrix
lora_A_weight = model.model.layers[0].self_attn.q_proj.lora_A["default"].weight
# Shape: (rank, in_features)

# Get lora B weight matrix
lora_B_weight = model.model.layers[0].self_attn.q_proj.lora_B["default"].weight
# Shape: (out_features, rank)

# Get scaling factor
scaling = model.model.layers[0].self_attn.q_proj.scaling["default"]
# Value: lora_alpha / r = 16 / 16 = 1.0 (by default)

# Check if adapters are enabled
is_enabled = not model.model.layers[0].self_attn.q_proj.disable_adapters

# Access active adapter name
active = model.model.layers[0].self_attn.q_proj.active_adapters[0]
# Usually: "default"
```

# Corrected function to save LoRA to a custom directory
def save_lora_to_custom_dir(model, tokenizer, save_directory):
    # Create the custom directory if it doesn't exist
    os.makedirs(save_directory, exist_ok=True)

    # Call the unsloth_save_model function with the custom directory
    unsloth_save_model(
        model,
        tokenizer,
        save_directory=save_directory,
        save_method="lora",
        push_to_hub=False,
    )

# Corrected method within the model class to convert LoRA to GGML and push to Hugging Face Hub
def unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub(
    self,
    tokenizer,
    repo_id: str,
    use_temp_dir: Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message: Optional[str] = "Converted LoRA to GGML with Unsloth",
    private: Optional[bool] = None,
    token: Union[bool, str, None] = None,
    create_pr: bool = False,
    revision: str = None,
    commit_description: str = "Convert LoRA to GGML format using Unsloth",
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
):
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda=False)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            python_install.wait()
    else:
        makefile = None

    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    lora_directory_push = "lora-to-ggml-push"
    save_lora_to_custom_dir(self, tokenizer, lora_directory_push)

    model_type = self.config.model_type
    output_file = os.path.join(lora_directory_push, "ggml-adapter-model.bin")

    print(f"Unsloth: Converting auto-saved LoRA adapters at {lora_directory_push} to GGML format.")
    print(f"The output file will be {output_file}")

    command = f"python3 llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py {lora_directory_push} {output_file} llama"

    try:
        with subprocess.Popen(command, shell=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE, bufsize=1, universal_newlines=True) as sp:
            for line in sp.stdout:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            for line in sp.stderr:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            sp.wait()
            if sp.returncode != 0:
                raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(sp.returncode, command)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        print(f"Error: Conversion failed with return code {e.returncode}")
        return

    print(f"Unsloth: Conversion completed! Output file: {output_file}")

    print("Unsloth: Uploading GGML file to Hugging Face Hub...")
    username = upload_to_huggingface(
        self, repo_id, token,
        "GGML converted LoRA", "ggml", output_file, None, private,
    )
    link = f"{repo_id.lstrip('/')}"
    print("Unsloth: Done.")
    print(f"Converted LoRA to GGML and uploaded to https://huggingface.co/{link}")
    print("\nThis GGML making function was made by Maheswar. Ping him @Maheswar on the Unsloth Discord or on HuggingFace (@mahiatlinux) if you like this!")

def unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally(
    self,
    save_directory: str, # Added parameter for the folder name
    tokenizer,
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
):
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda=False)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            python_install.wait()
    else:
        makefile = None

    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    # Use the provided save_directory for local saving
    save_lora_to_custom_dir(self, tokenizer, save_directory)

    model_type = self.config.model_type
    output_file = os.path.join(save_directory, "ggml-adapter-model.bin")

    print(f"Unsloth: Converting auto-saved LoRA adapters at {save_directory} to GGML format.")
    print(f"The output file will be {output_file}")

    command = f"python3 llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py {save_directory} {output_file} llama"

    try:
        with subprocess.Popen(command, shell=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE, bufsize=1, universal_newlines=True) as sp:
            for line in sp.stdout:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            for line in sp.stderr:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            sp.wait()
            if sp.returncode != 0:
                raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(sp.returncode, command)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        print(f"Error: Conversion failed with return code {e.returncode}")
        return
    print("Unsloth: Done.")
    print(f"Unsloth: Conversion completed! Output file: {output_file}")
    print("\nThis GGML making function was made by Maheswar. Ping him @Maheswar on the Unsloth Discord or on HuggingFace (@mahiatlinux) if you like this!")

from .models.loader_utils import get_model_name
from unsloth_zoo.saving_utils import (
    merge_and_overwrite_lora,
    prepare_saving,
)
from unsloth_zoo.llama_cpp import (
    install_llama_cpp,
    convert_to_gguf as _convert_to_gguf,
)

@torch.inference_mode
def save_to_gguf_generic(
    model,
    save_directory,
    tokenizer,
    quantization_method = None,
    quantization_type = "Q8_0",
    repo_id = None,
    token = None,
):
    if token is None and repo_id is not None: token = get_token()
    if repo_id is not None and token is None:
        raise RuntimeError("Unsloth: Please specify a token for uploading!")

    if not os.path.exists(os.path.join("llama.cpp", "unsloth_convert_hf_to_gguf.py")):
        install_llama_cpp(just_clone_repo = True)
    pass

    # Use old style quantization_method
    new_quantization_methods = []
    if quantization_method is not None:
        # Convert quantization_method to list
        if   isinstance(quantization_method, list):  pass
        elif isinstance(quantization_method, str):   quantization_method = [ quantization_method, ]
        elif isinstance(quantization_method, tuple): quantization_method = list(quantization_method)
        else:
            raise TypeError("Unsloth: quantization_method can only be a string or a list of strings")
        pass
        for i, quant_method in enumerate(quantization_method):
            quant_method = quant_method.lower()
            if   quant_method == "not_quantized":  quant_method = "f16"
            elif quant_method == "fast_quantized": quant_method = "q8_0"
            elif quant_method == "quantized":      quant_method = "q4_k_m"
            elif quant_method is None:             quant_method = "q8_0"
            new_quantization_methods.append(quant_method.lower())
        pass
    else:
        new_quantization_methods.append(quantization_type.lower())
    # Check if wrong method
    for quant_method in new_quantization_methods:
        if quant_method not in ALLOWED_QUANTS.keys():
            error = f"Unsloth: Quant method = [{quant_method}] not supported. Choose from below:\n"
            for key, value in ALLOWED_QUANTS.items():
                error += f"[{key}] => {value}\n"
            raise RuntimeError(error)

def not_implemented_save(*args, **kwargs):
    raise NotImplementedError("Unsloth: Sorry GGUF is currently not supported for vision models!")
pass


def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    import inspect
    import types
    from typing import Callable, Optional, Union, List

    # And now re add our saving methods!
    if model.push_to_hub.__name__ == "unsloth_push_to_hub":
        original_push_to_hub = model.original_push_to_hub
    else:
        original_push_to_hub = model.push_to_hub
    pass

    signature = str(inspect.signature(original_push_to_hub)).replace("NoneType", "None")
    signature = signature[1:]
    signature = re.sub("<function save at .+?>", "torch.save", signature)
    docs = original_push_to_hub.__doc__.encode("utf-8").decode("utf-8")

    push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
    """
    {docs}
    """
    arguments = dict(locals())
    del arguments["self"]
    if "tags" in arguments and arguments["tags"] is not None:
        assert(isinstance(arguments["tags"], (list, tuple)))
        arguments["tags"] = list(arguments["tags"]) + ["unsloth",]
    elif "tags" in arguments:
        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]
    elif hasattr(self, "add_model_tags"):
        self.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])

    if "commit_message" in arguments:
        commit_message = arguments["commit_message"]
        if commit_message is not None:
            if not commit_message.endswith(" "): commit_message += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
                commit_message += "(Trained with Unsloth)"
        else:
            commit_message = "Upload model trained with Unsloth"
        arguments["commit_message"] = commit_message

    if "commit_description" in arguments:
        commit_description = arguments["commit_description"]
        if commit_description is not None:
            if not commit_description.endswith(" "): commit_description += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_description:
                commit_description += "(Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
        else:
            commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
        arguments["commit_description"] = commit_description

    # Update model tag
    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        _ = upload_to_huggingface(
            self, arguments["repo_id"], arguments["token"],
            "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
            old_username = None, private = arguments["private"],
        )
    pass

    try:
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    except:
        del arguments["tags"]
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    pass

    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        print("Saved model to https://huggingface.co/" + arguments["repo_id"])
    pass
    '''
    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())

    original_model = model
    while True:

        if original_model.push_to_hub.__name__ != "unsloth_push_to_hub":
            original_model.original_push_to_hub = original_model.push_to_hub
            original_model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, original_model)
            if hasattr(original_model, "add_model_tags"):
                original_model.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])
            pass
        pass

        if hasattr(original_model, "model"): original_model = original_model.model
        else: break
    pass

    # Add saving methods to top level model
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            # Counteract tokenizers
            model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
            model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
            model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
        pass
    else:
        # Vision only 1 option
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,     model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,       model)
    pass
    return model
pass

def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    # For DPO or disabled adapters
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
    pass

    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        proj.lora_A [adapter].weight,
        proj.lora_B [adapter].weight,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
        base_layer.bias,
    )
pass


def _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X: torch.Tensor, proj: torch.nn.Module) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    If QAT is enabled, fake quantize the input activations.
    Otherwise, just return the input activations as is.
    Weights are fake quantized separately in `get_lora_parameters`.
    """
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    activation_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "activation_fake_quantizer", None)
    if activation_fake_quantizer is not None:
        X = activation_fake_quantizer(X)
    return X
pass

    @torch.inference_mode
    def fast_dequantize(W, quant_state = None, out = None, use_global_buffer = False):
        # TODO: After adding XPU BNB support, check this function
        if quant_state is None: return W
